data.title: Binance Japan Receives ISO/IEC 27001 and ISO/IEC 27701 Certifications
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News CZ forwarded the message from Richard Teng, CEO of Binance, on the X platform, saying that Binance Japan has obtained ISO/IEC 27001 and ISO/IEC 27701 certifications.

data.title: Stripe co-founder: A large part of the future payment volume will be in the form of stablecoins
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Joh Collison, co-founder and president of global payment company Stripe, said in an interview with Bloomberg that the company has had early discussions with banks on the integration of stablecoins, and said, "A large part of our future payment volume will be in the form of stablecoins, and banks are very interested in how to integrate stablecoins into their product systems. Banks will not ignore this, nor will they regard it as a temporary craze." (CoinDesk)

data.title: Potential Inflation Concerns Arise Amid Tariff Changes
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Nick Timiraos, known as the 'Fed's Mouthpiece,' highlighted two potential issues in the recently released inflation report. Analysts predict that with the implementation of 'Liberation Day' tariffs, the rate of increase in commodity prices will accelerate in May, particularly in June. Despite a cooling trend in inflation by mid-2024, the year-on-year comparison base is not advantageous, as evidenced by the U.S. core PCE price index recording an annual rate of 2.5% in April, marking the lowest since March 2021.

data.title: U.S. Consumer Spending Slows in April Amid Economic Concerns
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data indicates that U.S. consumer spending slowed in April following the strongest month of consumption since early 2023, while inflation remained moderate, aligning with the broader economic slowdown.

The core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index in the U.S. rose by 2.5% year-over-year in April, marking the smallest annual increase in over four years. This data highlights underlying anxiety among many American consumers after the weakest consumption quarter in nearly two years. Despite high tariffs on imported goods not yet widely impacting prices, consumer sentiment has significantly declined, and personal financial outlooks are at historic lows.

Another report released on Friday showed a significant narrowing of the U.S. goods trade deficit in April, driven by the largest recorded drop in import volumes, with goods imports falling by 19.8%, the largest decrease on record.

data.title: Federal Reserve's June Rate Decision Probabilities Analyzed
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the CME's FedWatch tool indicates a 5.4% probability of a 25 basis point rate cut by the Federal Reserve in June, while the likelihood of maintaining the current rate stands at 94.6%.

data.title: The Fed's "excellent" inflation report hides two major concerns
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Nick Timiraos, the "Federal Reserve mouthpiece", said that the "excellent" inflation report released today needs to pay attention to two potential problems: 
① Analysts expect that as the "Liberation Day" tariffs take effect, commodity price increases will accelerate in May (especially June). 
② Given that inflation has cooled in mid-2024, the year-on-year base itself is no longer advantageous (Note: The annual rate of the US core PCE price index in April was 2.5%, the lowest since March 2021). (Jinshi)

data.title: Stripe Engages in Early Talks with Banks for Stablecoin Global Payments
data.description: According to Odaily, payment service giant Stripe is in preliminary discussions with banks to explore the use of stablecoins for global payment solutions. Previously, Stripe announced the launch of stablecoin financial accounts, enabling users to send and receive funds through both fiat and cryptocurrency channels.

data.title: James Wynn: Currently only holds BTC, PEPE and moonpig
data.description: The whale James Wynn posted on the X platform that he has sold everything except BTC, PEPE and moonpig.

data.title: CryptoPunk 1831 sold for 150 ETH, worth nearly $400,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: CryptoSlam data shows that about 20 minutes ago, CryptoPunk 1831 was traded for 150 ETH, worth approximately US$393,000, and was also the NFT with the highest transaction volume in the past 24 hours.

data.title: Investor Concerns Rise in Bitcoin ETF Options Market
data.description: According to Odaily, the options market for BlackRock's spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund, iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), indicates growing investor concerns over downside risks. As of Thursday's close, the one-year put-call skew for IBIT turned positive, reaching 0.2%. This suggests that the premium for put options slightly exceeds that for call options, reflecting a shift in market sentiment from neutral to mildly bearish. On the same day, IBIT's share price fell by 1.32%, closing at $59.99. Despite this decline, the fund recorded a net inflow of $125 million, marking the lowest single-day inflow since May 13.

data.title: U.S. Trade Negotiations Unaffected by Tariff Litigation, Says Official
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Trade Representative Greer stated that ongoing tariff litigation has not impacted trade negotiations. Greer emphasized that if the tariff rulings take an unfavorable turn, there are alternative measures available to expand the scope of Section 301 tariffs as necessary.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise as Market Reacts
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. Treasury yields have increased, with the 2-year yield reaching 3.924% and the 10-year yield at 4.409%. This development reflects market reactions to current economic conditions.

data.title: BlackRock IBIT put premium exceeds call premium, market sentiment turns defensive
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The options market for BlackRock's spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) shows investors' concerns about downside risks. As of Thursday's close, IBIT's one-year put-call skew turned positive, reaching 0.2%, indicating that the premium for put options is slightly higher than that for call options, reflecting a shift in market sentiment from the previous neutral to slightly bearish. On the same day, IBIT's stock price fell 1.32% to close at $59.99, but still recorded a net inflow of $125 million, the lowest single-day inflow since May 13. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Traders still betting the Fed will cut rates in September
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Traders are still betting that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates in September. (Jinshi)

data.title: US Treasury yields set for monthly loss as markets wait on key Fed inflation data
data.description:
Treasury yields stayed still on Friday while traders waited on fresh inflation figures and followed President Donald Trump’s ongoing legal push to keep his reciprocal tariffs alive. The numbers barely moved — the 30-year yield ticked up by less than a basis point to 4. 927%, while the 10-year yield sat flat at 4. 422%, and the […]

data.title: U.S. Trade Representative Greer: Tariff litigation has not affected trade negotiations
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. Trade Representative Greer said that the tariff lawsuit did not affect the trade negotiations, and if the tariff ruling "goes in the opposite direction", we have other tools. (Jinshi)

data.title: BitMart launches Assisterr AI (ASRR)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch Assisterr AI (ASRR) at 22:00 (Eastern Time) on May 30, and the ASRR/USDT trading pair will be opened.
Assisterr combines tokenized professional language models (AI agents), real-time monetization, decentralized ownership, and agent-to-agent interoperability into a code-free, scalable platform.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures and Ethereum Experience Decline Amid Trade Remarks
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump recently made volatile trade-related comments on social media, leading to a further decline in major U.S. stock index futures. The Dow Jones futures fell by 0.5%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures both dropped approximately 0.7%. Additionally, Ethereum's price fell below $2,600 during the day, currently quoted at $2,590.

data.title: U.S. Trade Representative: The scope of Section 301 can be expanded as needed
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. Trade Representative Greer said that the scope of Section 301 tariff measures can be expanded as needed. Section 301 refers to the entire content of Sections 1301-1310 of the Comprehensive Trade and Competitiveness Act of 1988. Its main meaning is to protect the rights of the United States in international trade and retaliate against other countries whose trade practices are considered "unreasonable" and "unfair". According to this provision, the United States can investigate the trade practices of other countries that it considers to be "unfair", and can consult with the governments of the relevant countries. Finally, the president decides to take retaliatory measures such as raising tariffs, restricting imports, and stopping relevant agreements. (Jin Shi)
